message:
Deep-link URLs for the playground + RPC bench

The playground reads ?workload=&count=&iters= and writes them back via
history.replaceState whenever a selector changes. The RPC bench does
the same for ?iters=. Means visitors can share a URL pointing at a
specific bench config and the recipient sees those exact settings on
load (auto-run uses them).

Subtle: workload changes constrain the available counts (blob caps at
50), so applyUrlParams clamps before reading ?count= back. A stale
"?count=200" URL from a small-workload share won't get rejected when
the page loads with blob as default — it just clamps to 50.

Verified: deep-link initial state matches URL, changing the workload
selector reflects in the URL, blob auto-clamps the count.
